title: ranunculus bulbosus subsp. broteri
intro: Ranunculus bulbosus subsp. broteri, also known as Buttercup, is a perennial herb native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The plant's health benefits include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Ranunculus bulbosus subsp. broteri can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and rheumatism.
